mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals sustained labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through improved corporate earnings expectations. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2012 PAYEMS at 133,300 thousand persons indicates ongoing post-crisis labor recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth months do not produce significantly higher 20-day forward returns than negative growth months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25% and remains at the zero lower bound, the Fed signals maximum monetary accommodation. This threshold-triggered regime activates a positive equity signal because near-zero rates compress discount rates and force capital toward equities as alternative yield sources.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2012 FEDFUNDS at 0.13% confirms ZLB regime active
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: During ZLB periods (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%), 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% or t-stat < 1.5), or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding housing starts (HOUST) signals broad-based consumer demand strength. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive equity returns because joint expansion in consumption and residential investment indicates robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2012 RSAFS at 406,748 million dollars and HOUST at 746 thousand units show demand recovery
regime_hint: demand-led expansion with synchronized consumption and housing growth
failure_condition: Months with simultaneous RSAFS and HOUST growth do not produce significantly higher 20-day returns than months with only one or neither growing (t-test p > 0.10), or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1% month-over-month signals accelerating supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days because rapid production growth indicates strong corporate activity and near-term earnings surprises.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2012 INDPRO at 96.82 reflects post-crisis manufacturing recovery
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with accelerating capacity utilization
failure_condition: Months with INDPRO growth > 1% do not produce significantly higher 5-day forward returns than months with lower growth (t-test p > 0.15), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession risk. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings downward.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2012 UNRATE at 8.1% reflects elevated post-crisis unemployment
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: Periods with UNRATE increases > 0.3pp do not produce significantly lower 60-day forward returns than stable unemployment periods (t-test p > 0.10), or negative direction prediction accuracy < 55% in walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year signals monetary tightening pressure. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days because elevated inflation forces the Fed toward rate increases, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2012 CPIAUCSL at 230.10 reflects subdued post-crisis inflation
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation above target
failure_condition: High inflation periods (CPI YoY > 2.5%) do not produce significantly lower 20-day forward returns than low inflation periods (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion. This interaction-driven mechanism combines GDPC1 with PAYEMS growth: when both expand simultaneously, it confirms broad-based demand and labor market strength, predicting positive 60-day equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Q2 2012 GDPC1 at 13,564.5 billion chained dollars shows moderate growth
regime_hint: synchronized demand and labor expansion in mid-cycle recovery
failure_condition: Quarters with simultaneous GDPC1 growth > 2.5% and PAYEMS expansion do not produce significantly higher 60-day forward returns than quarters with only one or neither (t-test p > 0.10), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side contraction and falling capacity utilization. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days because production declines foreshadow earnings disappointments and recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2012 INDPRO at 96.82 reflects ongoing manufacturing recovery without sharp declines
failure_condition: Months with INDPRO declines > 0.5% do not produce significantly lower 20-day forward returns than months with stable or rising production (t-test p > 0.10), or negative direction prediction accuracy < 55% in walk-forward test.
